puzzles.title: Hello Function
puzzles.prompt: Complete the function so it returns the string 'Hello'.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function greet()
    -- TODO: return 'Hello'
end

print(greet())
puzzles.narrative: Still readable. Still manageable. One flower is facing the wrong way.
hints: functions return values with the return keyword
string literals use single or double quotes
return 'Hello'

puzzles.title: Sum Table Values
puzzles.prompt: Return the sum of all numeric values in the table.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function sum(tbl)
    -- TODO: sum values
end

print(sum({1,2,3,4}))
puzzles.narrative: Tables. Not arrays. Not lists. Tables. Something is different here.
hints: Lua tables are iterated with ipairs for sequential values
declare a local variable to accumulate the sum
for i, v in ipairs(tbl) do sum = sum + v end

puzzles.title: Reverse a String
puzzles.prompt: Reverse the input string without using built-in reverse helpers.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function reverse(s)
    -- TODO: reverse string manually
end

print(reverse("hello"))
puzzles.narrative: String indexing starts at 1 here. The ground shifted slightly.
hints: string.len(s) or #s gives you the length
string.sub(s, i, i) gets one character at position i
loop from #s down to 1 and concatenate

puzzles.title: Count Occurrences
puzzles.prompt: Count how many times 'x' appears in the string.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function countX(s)
    -- TODO: count occurrences of 'x'
end

print(countX("xoxooxxx"))
puzzles.narrative: Count the x's. There are more than you think. There are always more.
hints: string.gmatch can iterate over pattern matches
the pattern for a literal x is just 'x'
increment a counter for each match found

puzzles.title: Filter Table
puzzles.prompt: Return a new table containing only even numbers from the input.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function filterEven(tbl)
    -- TODO: build new table with even values
end

local result = filterEven({1,2,3,4,5,6})
print(table.concat(result, ","))
puzzles.narrative: Only the even ones survive. The odd ones are just gone now.
hints: create a new empty table with {}
use the modulo operator % to check for even numbers
table.insert(result, v) adds to the new table

puzzles.title: Closure Counter
puzzles.prompt: Fix the function so it returns a counter closure that increments each call.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function makeCounter()
    local n = 0
    -- TODO: return a function that increments and returns n
end

local c = makeCounter()
print(c())
print(c())
print(c())
puzzles.narrative: A function inside a function. The world is folding in on itself.
hints: return an anonymous function with return function() ... end
the inner function can see n from the outer scope
increment n then return it inside the inner function

puzzles.title: Coroutine Basics
puzzles.prompt: Fix the coroutine so it yields 1, then 2, then 3.
puzzles.starter_code:
local co = coroutine.create(function()
    -- TODO: yield 1, then 2, then 3
end)

for i = 1, 3 do
    local _, v = coroutine.resume(co)
    print(v)
end
puzzles.narrative: It pauses. It resumes. It pauses again. You are doing the same thing.
hints: coroutine.yield() suspends execution and returns a value
each resume picks up where the last yield left off
coroutine.yield(1) then coroutine.yield(2) then coroutine.yield(3)

puzzles.title: Deep Copy Table
puzzles.prompt: Implement a deep copy function that handles nested tables.
puzzles.hint: modifying copy should not affect original
puzzles.starter_code:
local function deepCopy(t)
    -- TODO: deep copy implementation
end

local original = {1, {2, 3}}
local copy = deepCopy(original)
copy[2][1] = 99

print(original[2][1])
puzzles.narrative: You made a copy. You changed the copy. The original changed anyway. Wait.
hints: a simple assignment copies the reference not the value
you need to recursively copy nested tables
check type(v) == 'table' before deciding how to copy each value

puzzles.title: Metatable Madness
puzzles.prompt: Fix the metatable so missing keys return 'missing' without breaking normal behavior.
puzzles.starter_code:
local function build()
    local t = { a = 1, b = 2 }

    local mt = {
        __index = function(tbl, key)
            -- TODO: return 'missing' ONLY when key does not exist
        end
    }

    setmetatable(t, mt)
    return t
end

local obj = build()
print(obj.a)
print(obj.c)
obj.d = 99
print(obj.d)
puzzles.narrative: The table knows what it doesn't have. Everything does now.
hints: __index is only called when the key is not found normally
rawget(tbl, key) checks the table without triggering metamethods
return rawget(tbl, key) or 'missing'
